name: M113 Armored Personnel Carrier
prompt:
Vietnam War era M113 APC "Green Dragon" - the ubiquitous armored box on tracks.

Physical structure:
- Hull: angular aluminum box shape, wider at bottom, sloped front
  - Front plate: angled armor plate ~60 degrees
  - Side plates: vertical, with slight outward angle at bottom
  - Rear: large drop-down ramp/door (shown closed, angled)
  - Top deck: flat with hatches
- Track system:
  - Drive sprocket: toothed wheel at front, ~0.3m radius
  - Idler wheel: at rear
  - Road wheels: 5 per side, smaller
  - Track: continuous band around wheels (simplified as box segments or single long box per side)
  - Track skirts: not present on M113
- Cupola: commander's cupola on top right front
  - M2 .50 cal machine gun mounted on cupola ring
  - Gun shield: small curved plate around gun
- Hatches: driver's hatch (front left), commander's hatch (cupola)
- Trim vane: folding bow plate at very front (for swimming), shown folded flat on glacis
- Antenna: whip antenna on rear right

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), dark tracks (0x333333), black road wheels (0x222222), gun metal (0x3a3a3a)
Orientation: Front toward +Z, sitting on tracks at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~4.9m long, ~2.7m wide, ~2.5m tall to cupola)
